source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Noble Stanley Lafosse Drillship Blackout and EDS Activation

context.region: Gulf of America, Mississippi Canyon Block 300

event.incident_type: Loss of station keeping leading to Emergency Disconnect Sequence activation and discharge of drilling fluids
actions_taken: Manual activation of Emergency Disconnect Sequence (EDS)
Detachment of Lower Marine Riser Package (LMRP)
Restoration of diesel generators and thrusters
Installation of barrier covers over emergency fuel shut off valves
event.summary: On March 28, 2025, an able body seaman (AB1) aboard the Noble Stanley Lafosse drillship mistakenly operated emergency fuel shut off valve switches instead of accommodation fire dampener switches during routine preventative maintenance. This error caused all diesel generators to trip sequentially, resulting in a total blackout and loss of dynamic positioning. The drillship drifted off station, triggering a manual Emergency Disconnect Sequence that sheared the drill pipe and released 2,436 barrels of drilling brine into the Gulf of America. The incident caused $2.6 million in damage to subsea equipment. Contributing factors included failure to conduct a required Task Risk Assessment, AB1 working alone without adequate supervision, self-perceived time pressure, and lack of physical barriers protecting critical emergency fuel shut off valves.
recommendations: Install barrier covers over emergency fuel shut off valves to prevent inadvertent operation
Ensure Task Risk Assessments are completed prior to all work tasks as per company policy
Provide adequate supervision and pairing of personnel for critical maintenance tasks
Reinforce Stop Work Authority culture and procedures
key_phrases: mistakenly operating the wrong switch panel
felt self-perceived pressure and rushed through the task
TRA was not performed until after the incident occurred
emergency fuel shut off valves were not protected with barriers or warning signs
Failure to execute Stop Work Authority
did not verify dampener line-item numbers

hazards.name: Pressurized drilling fluids in marine riser

threats.name: Inadvertent activation of emergency fuel shut off valves
Loss of diesel generator fuel supply

consequences.name: Loss of containment - discharge of drilling fluids to sea
Environmental pollution - 2,436 barrels of brine released
Equipment damage - BOP stack and PRT equipment
Safety risk to crew at sea

controls.name: Task Risk Assessment (TRA) procedure
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and verify work procedures before task execution
controls.lod_basis: Proactive hazard identification before work begins

supporting_text: the Task Risk Assessment (TRA), required prior to work, was not performed until after the incident occurred
Failure to conduct Task Risk Assessment (TRA) prior to commencement of job as per Noble Company policy requirement
AB1 was not provided with a Task Risk Assessment TRA as required in Noble policy prior to beginning the job
NSL personnel did not perform a TRA prior to commencement of job task in accordance with Noble company policy

controls.name: Stop Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Empower workers to halt work when uncertain or unsafe conditions exist
controls.lod_basis: Worker empowerment to prevent unsafe acts

supporting_text: Failure to execute Stop Work Authority when AB1 felt uncertainty regarding completing the tasks solo
AB1 conducted unsafe operations by not verifying dampener line-item numbers or using stop work authority when he felt unsure of required work task
NSL failed to execute Stop Work Authority as directed within the company policy

controls.name: Work instructions and line-item checklist for fire dampers
controls.barrier_role: Provide clear guidance and verification steps for maintenance tasks

supporting_text: Work instructions and line-item list of accommodation fire dampers used by AB1
AB1 conducted unsafe operations by not verifying dampener line-item numbers
NSL personnel did not verify item numbers at fire dampeners control panel

controls.name: Pairing of personnel for maintenance tasks
controls.barrier_role: Provide peer checking and support during critical maintenance

supporting_text: the Noble Drilling Company Able Body seaman 1 (AB1), who typically would be paired with another colleague, was tasked with testing the accommodation fire dampeners alone
No assistance from the NSL Bosun, also described as Marine Department Supervisor on duty

controls.name: Radio communication between AB1 and 2nd engineer
controls.barrier_role: Verify correct operation and position of dampers during testing

supporting_text: The Noble 2nd engineer located in the engine control room was responsible for confirming the closing and opening position of the dampeners through handheld radio communication
clear communications were not provided to AB1 who was relatively uncertain to work task

controls.name: Physical barriers on emergency fuel shut off valves
controls.barrier_role: Prevent inadvertent operation of critical emergency systems

supporting_text: the emergency fuel shut off valves were not protected with barriers or warning signs to prevent inadvertent functioning by ship personnel
rig management had installed barrier covers over the emergency fuel shut off valves, to mitigate future occurrences

controls.name: Warning signs on emergency fuel shut off valves
controls.barrier_role: Alert personnel to critical nature of equipment
controls.lod_basis: Visual warning to prevent inadvertent operation

supporting_text: the emergency fuel shut off valves were not protected with barriers or warning signs to prevent inadvertent functioning by ship personnel

controls.name: Equipment labeling at control panels
controls.barrier_role: Enable correct identification of equipment controls

supporting_text: Observation of the valves showed clear and legible labeling at each panel for the designated equipment

controls.name: Competency training for AB1
controls.barrier_role: Ensure personnel have knowledge and skills for assigned tasks

supporting_text: Noble AB1 employee: competency record
AB2 stated AB1 was knowledgeable in the assigned task of functioning accommodation fire dampeners
AB2 stated AB1 was familiar with the emergency gear locker room and the panels within for the fire dampeners
Despite having performed this task before

controls.name: Redundant diesel generators
controls.barrier_role: Maintain power generation capability if one generator fails
controls.lod_basis: Redundancy to prevent total power loss

supporting_text: 1634:27: Diesel Generator (DG) #5 in the starboard engine room tripped due to fuel loss
1634:53: DG #1 in the port engine room tripped
1635:47: DG #3 tripped shortly after the start attempt
1636:16: DG #4 tripped, leading to a full blackout

controls.name: Dynamic Positioning (DP) system with Yellow Alert
controls.barrier_role: Detect and alert operators to initial DP system degradation
controls.lod_basis: Early warning system for position keeping issues

supporting_text: 1634:41: A Dynamic Positioning (DP) Yellow Alert was activated, indicating initial operational concerns

controls.name: Dynamic Positioning (DP) Red Alert
controls.barrier_role: Alert operators to critical loss of position keeping requiring immediate action

supporting_text: 1636:46: A DP Red Alert activated, with the rig 7.5 meters from the setpoint
The senior DPO alerts rig floor of Red Zone Panel alert

controls.name: Emergency Disconnect Sequence (EDS)
controls.barrier_role: Disconnect riser and prevent well control loss during drift-off
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to prevent well control incident

supporting_text: the rig floor manually activated the Emergency Disconnect Sequence (EDS)
which sheared the 5.875 in., Z140, 34.21lb/ft drill pipe and successfully detached the Lower Marine Riser Package (LMRP)
the EDS was activated by rig floor personnel, unlatching 58 seconds later at 25.6 meters from the setpoint

controls.name: Blowout Preventer (BOP) stack
controls.barrier_role: Maintain well control and prevent uncontrolled release from well

supporting_text: Blow Out Preventer (BOP) Stack equipment damage due on seafloor drift

controls.name: Emergency power restoration procedures
controls.barrier_role: Restore power generation and dynamic positioning capability
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to restore critical systems

supporting_text: 1711:45: DG #1 (port engine room) restored online
1735:46: DG #6 (starboard engine room) restored online
1755:30: DG #4 (center engine room) restored online
1807:17: All six thrusters and three diesel generators fully operational, restoring DP control

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.

_validation_errors: bowtie -> controls -> 14 -> side: Input should be 'prevention' or 'mitigation'
